verification_notes: LOCATION MISMATCH: Kumlung-Ake Golf Course is confirmed by all sources (1golf.eu, Aloeus, Expedia, Thailandguide24) to be located in Mueang Loei District, Loei Province, northern Thailand — approximately 500 km from Bangkok (roughly 6–7 hours drive). This is NOT a Bangkok-area course. Pipeline brief listed it as 'Near Muang Ake area' which appears to be an error — the 'Ake' in the name is coincidental. The course is a 9-hole military/public facility at Sri Song Ruk Camp, Loei. Processed here per pipeline instructions but flagged prominently. Green fees reportedly very low (~100–300 THB range, older data). Significant data gaps due to extremely limited web presence.
disputes: MAJOR: Course is in Loei province (~500 km from Bangkok), not in the Bangkok/Pathum Thani area implied by the pipeline brief. The 'Kumlung Ake' name is unrelated to the Bangkok-area 'Muang Ake' golf group.
Course details (par, yards) not independently confirmed — 9 holes is confirmed; par and yards are model estimates.
Green fees: older reviews cite 310 THB for 18 holes including caddie (9 holes played twice). May be significantly outdated.

prose.overview: Kumlung-Ake Golf Course is a nine-hole public golf facility at Sri Song Ruk Military Camp in Mueang Loei District, Loei Province — in northern Thailand, approximately 500 kilometres from Bangkok. Note that despite the 'Ake' suffix, this course has no connection to the Muang Ake golf group in Pathum Thani; the name is coincidental. The course is a modest, grassroots facility built on military land and open to the public, serving the local Loei population who otherwise have limited golf access in this mountainous northern province. With reportedly very affordable green fees (around 310 THB including caddie for a full 18-hole round played across the 9 holes), it serves as a community amenity rather than a visitor destination. The surrounding Loei Province is notable for its cool mountain climate — the coldest temperatures in Thailand occur here during winter months — and the provincial capital is known for Phu Kradueng National Park.
prose.layout_and_experience: The nine-hole layout is set on relatively flat meadowland terrain within the military camp grounds, surrounded by trees and modest landscape features characteristic of the Loei valley. As a nine-hole facility, a full round requires playing the holes twice. Course conditioning is consistent with a community public facility — functional but not at resort standards. The primary appeal is accessibility and price: caddies are available and club hire on-site means golfers can play without any equipment. The course is infrequently visited by international tourists, making it a genuinely local experience with all the social character that implies.
prose.tips: Kumlung-Ake is primarily a local community facility and visiting Bangkok-based golfers are unlikely to make the 6–7 hour drive specifically for this course. For golfers already in Loei — visiting Phu Kradueng, Phu Rua National Park, or the Loei Phantom Festival — a round here is a pleasant and inexpensive addition. Club hire at approximately 300 THB makes it easy to play without carrying clubs. Phone the club in advance (+66 42 811 939) to confirm current operating hours, as information is limited online.
prose.location_and_access: Kumlung-Ake Golf Course is located at Km. 10, Loei–Chiang Khan Road, Sri Song Ruk Camp, Mueang Loei District, Loei 42110. The course is approximately 500 km from Bangkok, a drive of 6–7 hours via Phitsanulok and Loei Highway. The nearest airport is Loei Airport (LOE) served by domestic flights from Bangkok (Don Mueang). No international tourist infrastructure serves the course; it is a local facility.
prose.rental_cta_context: If you are making the long journey to Loei province for its mountain parks and cool climate, LENGOLF can ensure quality clubs are ready for you in Bangkok before you depart — eliminating one logistical concern on an extended northern Thailand road trip.
